stem: A HR analytics study surveys 20 staff members and observes 4 positive outcomes out of 20 (20.0%). The sample contains two subgroups: Group A (7 observations, rate = 14.3%) and Group B (13 observations, rate = 23.1%). The true population composition is uncertain: Group A makes up between 38% and 54% of the population. Estimate the true employee turnover rate and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Simple pooled estimate: 0.2. Weighted estimate (stratified): 0.2097 (range [0.2061, 0.2134]). Combined CI accounting for stratification uncertainty: [0.0807, 0.416]. Point estimate: 0.2097.

solution_steps: Stratified estimate: weighted by population composition. At w_A=0.46: 0.46×0.185 + 0.54×0.2308 = 0.2097.
common_failure_modes: Using the Wald interval (p̂ ± z√(p̂(1-p̂)/n)) which performs poorly for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
Not recognizing that with n=20, the normal approximation may be poor
Ignoring the population composition uncertainty and using the simple pooled rate
